reasoning: Wagner Moura officially received a Best Actor Oscar nomination for his role in 'The Secret Agent', winning Best Actor at Cannes and the Golden Globe for Motion Picture - Drama. However, he failed to receive a nomination from the Screen Actors Guild (SAG Awards) and also missed the BAFTA longlist/shortlist entirely. In the history of the Academy Awards, no lead actor has ever won an Oscar without a SAG nomination. Furthermore, Timothee Chalamet heavily dominates the race, having won the Critics Choice Award and Golden Globe (Comedy). With no major industry guild (SAG, BAFTA) support outside the Golden Globes, his chances are extremely marginal. An upset isn't technically impossible but approaches statistical zero. I forecast a 4% chance based on general noise and a non-zero tail-risk of international voters breaking precedent.
